name: ALLIANT ENERGY CORP
business: Alliant Energy operates as a regulated investor-owned public utility holding company, providing regulated electric and natural gas service to approximately 1,010,000 electric and approximately 435,000 natural gas customers in the Midwest through its two public utility subsidiaries, IPL and WPL. It also owns non-utility holdings including a 16% ownership interest in ATC, an independent transmission-only company, and a 50% interest in a 225 MW non-utility wind farm.
sellsTo: retail electric and natural gas customers (residential, commercial, industrial) in Iowa and Wisconsin
wholesale electric customers (municipalities, rural electric cooperatives) in Iowa, Illinois, and Wisconsin
gas transportation service to commercial and industrial customers

exposures.sentence: Alliant Energy operates as a regulated investor-owned public utility holding company, and its purpose-driven strategy is to serve its customers and build stronger communities.
ATC Holdings is comprised of a 16% ownership interest in ATC and a 20% ownership interest in ATC Holdco LLC. ATC is an independent, for-profit, transmission-only company.
Alliant Energy's industry-leading wind and solar energy resources provide zero-fuel cost generation as well as generate renewable tax credits that are provided to its electric customers.
Completion of construction of energy storage projects totaling 175 MW at WPL and 99MW at IPL in 2025.

IPL has entered into electric service agreements with two new customers, and WPL has entered into an electric service agreement with one new customer, each of whom is constructing or expects to construct one or more data centers in IPL's or WPL's service territories.
A cyber attack may disrupt our operations or lead to a loss or misuse of confidential and proprietary information or potential liability.

catalysts.event: IPL retail electric base rate moratorium begins
catalysts.sentence: IPL maintaining flat base rates for its retail electric and gas customers from 2021 through September 30, 2024, as well as a retail electric base rate moratorium from October 2025 through September 2029 approved by the IUC in IPL's most recent retail electric rate review.

catalysts.event: WPL retail electric rate increase effective January 1, 2026
catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, the PSCW issued an order authorizing annual base rate increases of $69 million and $7 million for WPL's retail electric and gas customers, respectively, effective January 1, 2026, for the 2026 forward-looking Test Period.

catalysts.event: WPL additional retail electric rate increase effective January 1, 2027
catalysts.sentence: The PSCW's order also authorized WPL to implement an additional $75 million and $5 million increase in annual rates for its retail electric and gas customers, respectively, effective January 1, 2027, for the 2027 forward-looking Test Period.

catalysts.event: Neenah Unit 2 and Sheboygan Falls Unit 2 advanced gas path projects expected completion
catalysts.sentence: The Neenah Unit 2 and Sheboygan Falls Unit 2 advanced gas path projects are expected to be completed in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: Whispering Willow - East wind farm refurbishment expected completion
catalysts.sentence: IPL currently expects the refurbishment of the existing Whispering Willow - East wind farm to be completed in 2026.

catalysts.event: IUC decision on advance rate-making principles for up to 1,000 MW wind generation expected
catalysts.sentence: A decision from the IUC is currently expected in the first half of 2026.

catalysts.event: PSCW decision on WPL's individual customer rate for data center expected
catalysts.sentence: A decision from the PSCW is currently expected in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: PSCW decision on WPL's CA application for Bent Tree North wind farm expected
catalysts.sentence: A decision from the PSCW is currently expected in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: PSCW decision on WPL's liquified natural gas facility expected
catalysts.sentence: A decision from the PSCW is currently expected in the second quarter of 2026.

catalysts.event: EPA final rule on repeal of CAA Section 111(d) expected
catalysts.sentence: In June 2025, the EPA proposed to repeal CAA Section 111(d) and a final rule is expected in the first half of 2026.
